observations.recommendation:
Require the City Accountant to:

Cease from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance given to an accountable officer is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made, and withhold the salaries of all accountable officers who are still connected with the LGU until settlement of their respective cash advances;

Send demand letters and instruct all accountable officers with outstanding unliquidated cash advances to liquidate their cash advances within 15 days from receipt hereof, or else refund the full amount advanced, otherwise, administrative, and criminal prosecution may be sought by management and/or the audit team; and

Fast track the conduct of the verification, analysis and validation on the existence of those dormant accounts as a basis for the application of the request for write off following the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2024-008.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant's Office has diligently monitored the utilization of cash advances, and accountable officers are regularly reminded of their responsibility to liquidate the advances promptly. Demand letters are issued to enforce compliance with liquidation requirements. Out of the ₱33.439M unliquidated balance, only ₱397,290 remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2024. Of this, ₱328,062.74 was liquidated on January 16, 2025 while the remaining of ₱69,227.40 has no supporting documents available.
observations.reason:
The LGU encountered challenges with the limited number of disbursing officers available to handle the disbursement processes, particularly in light of the simultaneous implementation of multiple programs as well as releasing of honorariums and incentives to individuals. 

The submitted Schedules of Dormant Unliquidated Cash Advance were returned to the agency for submission of the Request for Authority to Write- Off duly filed by the Head of the Agency and submission of additional supporting documents.

Reiterated with revisions in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to enjoin the City Accountant to fast track the conduct of the verification, analysis and validation on the existence of those dormant accounts as basis for the application of the request for write-off following the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2024-008.

Reiterated as part of finding no. 1 of Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: 1.8	We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to revert the recorded costs of the derecognized PPE and accumulated depreciation thereof in the books of accounts and strictly adhere to the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

2.8	We recommended to the management to:
Direct the City Engineer to obtain copies of the turnover documents on the above-mentioned projects from the DPWH and provide them to the City Accountant for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to record the assets in the books of the City for the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Ensure that turnover documents are obtained for projects funded and implemented by other government agencies when the City assumes control over them for proper recognition in the books of the City.

4.9	We recommended to the management to:
Require the City Accountant to prepare journal entry voucher (JEV) to recognize as ‘Accounts Payable’ all unpaid goods and services received as at December 31, 2024; and
Enjoin the General Services Office to submit all unpaid POs and AIRs for goods and services received before the end of the year to the City Accountant’s Office prior to the cut-off period of the financial statements to ensure proper and timely recognition of liabilities.

5.6	We recommended that management require the City Accountant to:
Conduct a comprehensive reconciliation of the ‘Due from Other Funds’ and ‘Due to Other Funds’ accounts across all funds, and prepare corresponding adjusting entries supported by complete and valid documentation. Also, ensure that fund transfers are made to the appropriate bank accounts to resolve any identified reconciling items; and
Implement a structured and periodic monitoring system of the intra-agency accounts to prevent the recurrence of errors or deficiencies and accumulation of unadjusted reconciling items.

6.10	We recommended to the management the following:
Instruct the City Accountant to follow up with the concerned barangays and other LGUs for the immediate   submission of Fund Utilization Reports, and to require the return of any unutilized funds from completed projects or activity as prescribed under COA Circular No. 94-013;
Enjoin the City Accountant to examine both barangay and city accounting records to identify the disbursements charged against the fund transfers, in order to assist the barangays in tracing the necessary documents for the preparation of their Fund Utilization Reports; and
Consider establishing a sound internal control policy that limits the release of additional funds to barangays or other entities with outstanding unliquidated fund transfers.

8.9	We recommended to the management to:
8.9.1	Fast-track the approval of the Physical Inventory Plan and submit to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities;
8.9.2	Prioritize implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
8.9.3	Require the Inventory Committee to ensure the conduct of an annual physical inventory of PPE, preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and its submission on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.

11.12	We recommended to the management to:
11.12.1	Instruct the City Engineering Office to include the cost of obtaining building permits as a pay item in the Program of Works (POW);
11.12.2	Require the contractors, in future vertical projects, to secure a building permit prior to construction and a Certificate of Occupancy prior to occupancy and use by the end-user; and
11.12.3	Consider establishing a separate division under the Office of the City Engineer or creating an Office of the City Building Official that is separate and distinct from the Office of the City Engineer, to be headed by qualified technical personnel other than the City Engineer, in accordance with the qualifications outlined in PD 1096 and who is not involved in the implementation of government projects, to avoid conflicts of interest and to have a check and balance mechanism.

Cease from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance given to an accountable officer is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made, and withhold the salaries of all accountable officers who are still connected with the LGU until settlement of their respective cash advances;
Send demand letters and instruct all accountable officers with outstanding unliquidated cash advances to liquidate their cash advances within 15 days from receipt hereof, or else refund the full amount advanced, otherwise, administrative, and criminal prosecution may be sought by management and/or the audit team; and
Fast track the conduct of the verification, analysis and validation on the existence of those dormant accounts as a basis for the application of the request for write off following the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

3.8	We recommended that management:
Instruct the City Engineer and the City Accountant to have periodic coordination and reconciliation relative to the Quarterly Reports of Government Projects and the latter be furnished copy of the reports as bases for proper accrual of liabilities and recognition in the books of accounts of the city’s CIP projects and subsequent reclassification to PPEs; and
Require the City Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to record the accrual of liabilities on works accomplished as of year-end.

4.7	We recommended to management to enjoin the City Accountant to fast track the conduct of the verification, analysis and validation on the existence of those dormant accounts as basis for the application of the request for write-off following the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

6.9.1	Advice the City Accountant and CHRMO to confer with GSIS on the reconciliation of unpaid premiums per individual accounts and prepare adjustments to reflect the correct account balance in the books of the city; and

Direct the Budget Officer to clearly define, prioritize and implement infrastructure projects charged to 20% Development Fund based on approved budget. Otherwise, ineligible claims will be disallowed in audit.
Instruct the management that projects should be implemented as planned and in consonance with the Annual Investment & Development Plan of the agency to ensure regularity, transparency, economy, efficiency and effectiveness.

We recommended to management the following:
Direct the City Accountant to update subsidiary ledgers provided for each account as well as the controlling account in the general ledger for easy verification and control of the accounts, to facilitate review of transactions and events recorded and to obtain reliability of financial records and reports; and
Improve the present computerized system of the accounting office that will      provide adequate data control and present accurate reports.

Recommendations:
We have recommended that the management should  submit legal basis in the payment of such honorarium otherwise payment hereof should be disallowed in audit; and
Follow strictly the provisions of Section 4, PD 1445 and Section 3.2 of the DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-02 dated October 1, 2007.

We recommended the following:
Require the accounting unit to submit the lacking delivery receipts as soon as possible otherwise, the transaction shall be suspended in the audit;
Require the management to stop the practice to spend to any religious sect out of   government funds, it contravenes Section 29(2), Article VI, 1987 Philippine Constitution; and
Require the management that should take full cognizance of their responsibility and accountability relative to the prudent use of scarce financial resources, strict compliance with the existing budgeting, accounting and auditing rules and regulations, specifically COA Circular No. 2012-003 on the prevention of the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures or uses of government funds and property.

We recommended the following:
Direct the accountable officers/persons in charge to liquidate cash advances upon completion of the purpose for which cash advances were granted. Otherwise, these transactions will be subject to suspensions; and
Direct the Accounting Unit to act immediately on liquidation documents for processing and recording in the books.

We recommended to –
Require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that GPB is appropriately evaluated by the DILG to guarantee that the P/P/As to be undertaken are part of the GAD priority agenda of the government and that activities are sufficiently funded.
Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the annual GAD plan with the different departments responsible for implementing projects and activities relative to gender and development to ensure that activities identified are in accordance with the target activities of these implementing departments.
Require the GAD Focal Person to coordinate with the Budget Office on budget matters during the actual implementation to ensure that the financial plan is efficiently carried out;
Require the GAD Focal Person to conduct regular monitoring of the GAD Plan to ensure that planned activities are accomplished.
Require the GAD Focal Person to coordinate with the PCW for the implementation of the Annual GAD Plan Budget and the GAD Work Plan for the incoming years.
